title: MarginalizationAK
lean_code_with_comments:
def MarginalizationΦE (G : CDMG Node) (W : Finset Node) (u v : Node) : Prop :=
  ∃ (p : Walk G u v),
    p.IsDirectedWalk ∧
    p.length ≥ 1 ∧
    (∀ x ∈ p.vertices.tail.dropLast, x ∈ W) ∧
    (u = v → p.length ≥ 2)

def MarginalizationΦL (G : CDMG Node) (W : Finset Node) (u v : Node) : Prop :=
  (∃ (p : Walk G u v),
      p.IsBifurcation ∧ ∀ x ∈ p.vertices.tail.dropLast, x ∈ W) ∨
  (∃ (p : Walk G v u),
      p.IsBifurcation ∧ ∀ x ∈ p.vertices.tail.dropLast, x ∈ W)

noncomputable def marginalize (G : CDMG Node) (W : Finset Node)
    (hW : W ⊆ G.V) : CDMG Node where
  J := G.J
  V := G.V \ W
  hJV_disj := marginalize_hJV_disj G W
  E := ((G.J ∪ (G.V \ W)) ×ˢ (G.V \ W)).filter
        (fun e => G.MarginalizationΦE W e.1 e.2)
  hE_subset := marginalize_hE_subset G W
  L := ((G.V \ W) ×ˢ (G.V \ W)).filter
        (fun e => e.1 ≠ e.2 ∧ G.MarginalizationΦL W e.1 e.2)
  hL_subset := marginalize_hL_subset G W
  hL_irrefl := marginalize_hL_irrefl G W
  hL_symm := marginalize_hL_symm G W
lean_code_without_comments:
def MarginalizationΦE (G : CDMG Node) (W : Finset Node) (u v : Node) : Prop :=
  ∃ (p : Walk G u v),
    p.IsDirectedWalk ∧
    p.length ≥ 1 ∧
    (∀ x ∈ p.vertices.tail.dropLast, x ∈ W) ∧
    (u = v → p.length ≥ 2)

def MarginalizationΦL (G : CDMG Node) (W : Finset Node) (u v : Node) : Prop :=
  (∃ (p : Walk G u v),
      p.IsBifurcation ∧ ∀ x ∈ p.vertices.tail.dropLast, x ∈ W) ∨
  (∃ (p : Walk G v u),
      p.IsBifurcation ∧ ∀ x ∈ p.vertices.tail.dropLast, x ∈ W)

noncomputable def marginalize (G : CDMG Node) (W : Finset Node)
    (hW : W ⊆ G.V) : CDMG Node where
  J := G.J
  V := G.V \ W
  hJV_disj := marginalize_hJV_disj G W
  E := ((G.J ∪ (G.V \ W)) ×ˢ (G.V \ W)).filter
        (fun e => G.MarginalizationΦE W e.1 e.2)
  hE_subset := marginalize_hE_subset G W
  L := ((G.V \ W) ×ˢ (G.V \ W)).filter
        (fun e => e.1 ≠ e.2 ∧ G.MarginalizationΦL W e.1 e.2)
  hL_subset := marginalize_hL_subset G W
  hL_irrefl := marginalize_hL_irrefl G W
  hL_symm := marginalize_hL_symm G W
lean_explanation:
The Lean encoding of marginalization on a CDMG is split into three top-level declarations: two membership predicates (`MarginalizationΦE`, `MarginalizationΦL`) and the main `def marginalize`.

`Node : Type*` is the ambient vertex type, and `[DecidableEq Node]` supplies decidable node equality — required for `Finset`-backed products (`×ˢ`) and filters on edge sets. Both are introduced once via a `variable` block so they auto-bind into every declaration in this file.

`MarginalizationΦE G W u v` reads `∃ p : Walk G u v, ...`: there exists a walk from `u` to `v` such that (i) it is directed (`p.IsDirectedWalk`), (ii) it has `p.length ≥ 1`, (iii) every intermediate vertex `x ∈ p.vertices.tail.dropLast` lies in `W`, and (iv) the self-cycle restriction `u = v → p.length ≥ 2` is satisfied. The list idiom `p.vertices.tail.dropLast` extracts the intermediate slice $[w_1, \ldots, w_{n-1}]$ by dropping the head $w_0 = u$ and the tail $w_n = v$, matching the LN's "if any" qualifier (the slice is empty when $n = 1$).

`MarginalizationΦL G W u v` is a disjunction over the two walk orientations: either `∃ p : Walk G u v, p.IsBifurcation ∧ ...` or the same with `Walk G v u`. `Walk.IsBifurcation` (from `def_3_4`) already packages clauses (a)–(f) of the LN bifurcation structure — the split-index hinge, the left and right arms, the end-node arrowhead constraint — so the structural content is delegated there and only the intermediates-in-`W` conjunct is added here.

`marginalize` returns a `CDMG Node` via named-field `where` syntax. The four data fields map directly onto the LN's clauses i–iv: `J := G.J`, `V := G.V \ W`, and the two edge sets as `Finset.filter`s over the product carriers `(G.J ∪ (G.V \ W)) ×ˢ (G.V \ W)` and `(G.V \ W) ×ˢ (G.V \ W)`. The five `CDMG` proof obligations of `def_3_1` (input/output disjointness, edge carriers, bidirected irreflexivity, bidirected symmetry) are discharged by five named private lemmas above the def. The hypothesis `hW : W ⊆ G.V` enforces the LN's "$W \subseteq V$" precondition. The whole declaration is `noncomputable`, inherited from the classical `Decidable` instances on `Φ_E` and `Φ_L`.
design_choices:
**Two membership predicates factored out as named `def`s, not inlined into `marginalize`.** Every downstream claim about marginalized edges reasons by unfolding "membership in `(G.marginalize W).E` / `…L`" to "exists a witnessing walk through `W`". A named predicate carries that unfolding step as a single rewrite plus dot-notation API; inlining the four-conjunct existential into the structure literal would force ad-hoc destructuring on every consumer.

**Reuse `Walk G u v` as the witness type, not a bespoke `List Node` enumeration.** The chapter has already paid the foundational cost of the `Walk` inductive in `def_3_4`; it carries the LN's vertex-edge alternation with the per-step constraint built in, and downstream definitions (ancestors, acyclicity, the bifurcation alternative) already consume walks uniformly. A bespoke list reformulation would force every marginalization claim to translate back to the `Walk` API, doubling the work for no gain.

**Symmetric `Φ_L` via `Or` over both walk orientations.** A bifurcation between `u` and `v` is semantically symmetric, but `Walk G u v` and `Walk G v u` are distinct Lean types and there is no general `Walk.reverse` operation in the chapter yet (only the directed-walk special case in `BifurcationAlternative.lean`). Building the disjunction into `Φ_L` makes its symmetry evident: the `hL_symm` obligation on the marginalized graph's bidirected edges closes by `Or.comm` rather than requiring a generic walk-reversal infrastructure that the chapter has not paid for.

**Self-cycle asymmetry between $E^{\setminus W}$ and $L^{\setminus W}$ preserved exactly.** The LN intentionally distinguishes the two channels: directed self-cycles $(v, v) \in E^{\setminus W}$ are admitted when witnessed by a directed walk of length $\ge 2$ through `W`, but bidirected self-edges $(v, v) \in L^{\setminus W}$ are excluded outright. The encoding mirrors this exactly. `Φ_E` carries the conjunct `u = v → p.length ≥ 2` (the length-restricted admission for directed self-cycles); the `L^{\setminus W}` set-builder carries an explicit `e.1 ≠ e.2` filter conjunct (the irreflexivity exclusion for bidirected self-edges, also load-bearing for `hL_irrefl`).

**Classical decidability and `noncomputable`.** The membership predicates quantify existentially over walks of arbitrary length. A constructive decision procedure would require either a vertex-count bound on walk length or a `Finset`-based reachability fixpoint — substantial new infrastructure that no downstream row currently needs, because the marginalization claims reason set-theoretically about *which pairs* lie in the edge sets rather than by running a decision procedure. Two per-element `Classical.propDecidable` instances are sufficient; the `noncomputable` annotation propagates to `marginalize` automatically.

**`hW : W ⊆ G.V` as an explicit but unused precondition.** The LN's "$W \subseteq V$" is structural: marginalising input nodes is meaningless, because by `def_3_1` no edge of a CDMG has an input as its target, so input-node marginalisation has no well-defined edge behaviour. The hypothesis is carried purely for signature faithfulness to the LN; none of the five proof obligations consume it, and a `linter.unusedVariables` exemption suppresses the unused-argument warning.
